equipment_diagnostics.name: get_equipment_status
equipment_diagnostics.description: Get current real-time metrics (inverter power, battery SOC, grid state, alarms) for a specific site.

equipment_diagnostics.name: get_site_info
equipment_diagnostics.description: Retrieve high-level site metadata: online/offline status, phase configuration, and location.

equipment_diagnostics.name: get_historical_power_data
equipment_diagnostics.description: Analyze historical power trends, peak loads, or outage patterns (up to 90 days).

equipment_diagnostics.name: get_batch_downtime_summary
equipment_diagnostics.description: Efficiently check 24-hour downtime stats for multiple grids in a single parallel call.

equipment_diagnostics.name: analyze_grid_outage
equipment_diagnostics.description: Analyze a specific grid outage event in detail. Identifies cause (battery depletion, overload, temperature), affected phases, load at failure, battery state, related alarms, and recovery pattern. If no outage_time is given, analyzes the most recent outage in the last 24 hours.

outage_time.description: Approximate time of outage (ISO datetime). If not provided, finds the most recent outage.

jira.name: jira_search_issues_with_comments
jira.description: List and filter multiple Jira issues i.e. tickets. Use for 'my tickets', 'open tickets', or searching by grid/org. Does NOT provide details for a single ticket. By default excludes Done tickets unless statuses are explicitly provided or exclude_done is set to false. When presenting results, always include the created date in short format (e.g. '15 Mar') alongside each ticket.

statuses.description: Filter by Jira statuses. Valid values: 'To Do', 'In Progress', 'Done'. Note: there is no 'Open' status — omit statuses to get all non-done tickets.

assignee.description: Assignee name, email, 'me', or 'unassigned'. Omit for any assignee.

exclude_done.description: Exclude Done tickets. Defaults to true when no statuses provided, false when statuses are explicitly set.

jira.name: jira_get_issue
jira.description: Retrieve the full details, history, and comments of one specific Jira issue i.e. ticket. Use ONLY when a Ticket Key (e.g., OPS-123) is known.

jira.name: jira_get_ticket_statistics
jira.description: Get aggregated ticket statistics for the last N days (open + closed). Returns tickets per grid, top ticket types, and grids with above-average ticket counts. Use for executive summaries.

days.description: Number of days to look back. Default: 30

jira.name: jira_get_on_call
jira.description: Get the JSM on-call schedule for a date range — use for any question about who was, is, or will be on call. Works for past dates (e.g. 'last weekend'), present, and future. Pass start_date as YYYY-MM-DD or ISO 8601.

start_date.description: Start date in YYYY-MM-DD or ISO 8601 format (e.g. '2026-04-18')

end_date.description: End date (inclusive). Defaults to start_date if omitted.

jira.name: jira_add_comment
jira.description: [ACTION] Add a new comment to a specific Jira ticket.

jira.name: jira_change_status
jira.description: [ACTION] Change the status of a Jira ticket (e.g., close, start progress, reopen). Use transition names like 'Done', 'In Progress', 'To Do'. If the ticket is unassigned, it will be auto-assigned to the requesting user.

jira.name: jira_assign_issue
jira.description: [ACTION] Assign or reassign a Jira ticket to a user. Use 'me' to self-assign, 'unassigned' to remove assignee, or a person's name/email for fuzzy matching. Returns an error if the user is not found in Jira.

codebase.name: search_codebase
codebase.description: Find specific code patterns or regex matches across the repository.

codebase.name: search_code_semantic
codebase.description: Natural language search for concepts like 'how does auth work' using vector embeddings.

codebase.name: get_recent_production_prs
codebase.description: Get recent Pull Requests merged to production. Identifies if recent code changes caused behavioral differences.

codebase.name: get_pr_file_changes
codebase.description: Get detailed file changes for a specific PR number. Shows exactly what code was modified.

codebase.name: get_file_git_history
codebase.description: Get git commit history for a specific file. Shows when it was changed, by whom, and why.

codebase.name: investigate_database
codebase.description: Run a read-only SQL query against the Auth database for investigation. Only SELECT queries on allowed tables (meters, orders, grids, organizations, accounts). Max 100 rows, 10s timeout.

sql_query.description: SELECT query to execute against the Auth database

logs.name: fetch_logs_from_loki
logs.description: Retrieve raw software/application logs from Grafana Loki using LogQL queries.

logs.name: search_logs_semantic
logs.description: Semantic/concept search for logs. Use to find issues by description like 'database timeout' or 'payment error'.

meters.name: get_meter_dcu_status
meters.description: [READ-ONLY] Check if a meter's gateway or DCU is currently online. Does not ping the meter hardware.

meters.name: create_meter_reading_task
meters.description: [ACTION] Actively ping a physical meter for live data (voltage, credit). Incurs a 15-20s delay.

equipment_control.name: restart_inverter
equipment_control.description: [CRITICAL ACTION] Physically reboot inverter hardware. Use only after confirming no recurring electrical shorts.

equipment_control.name: restart_comms_chain
equipment_control.description: [ACTION] Reboot communication hardware (Cerbo, DCU, Router). Site will be offline for ~10 minutes.

customer.name: meter_information
customer.description: [READ-ONLY] Get customer account details, credit balance, and recent token history for a specific meter.

customer.name: customer_get_meter_consumption
customer.description: [READ-ONLY] Get daily consumption history for a meter over a time range (default 30 days). Returns a chart image and daily totals/max values from hourly snapshots. Use when the user asks about a meter's usage, consumption pattern, or energy history.

days_back.description: Number of days to look back (default 30, max 365)

customer.name: customer_get_grid_chat_chronology
customer.description: [READ-ONLY] Get a chronological timeline of all chat messages related to a specific grid — from its O&M group topic, individual org users, and developer groups. Use when asked about communication history, customer complaints, or recent discussions about a grid. Accepts grid name OR organization name (e.g., 'AcmeCorp' resolves to its grid 'ExampleGrid'). Returns annotated messages with source context.

grid_name.description: Grid name or organization name (supports fuzzy matching)

days_back.description: Number of days to look back (default 7, max 90)

customer.name: customer_list_grid_meters
customer.description: [READ-ONLY] List all meters for a grid with their power limits, HPS mode presets, status, credit balance, and phase count. Excludes cabin/AC meters. Use this when the user asks about meters across a grid — e.g., which meters have a specific power limit, how many meters are on/off, or bulk meter status. Supports fuzzy grid name matching.

customer.name: customer_get_meters_on_pole
customer.description: [READ-ONLY] List all meters connected to a specific pole. Use when the user asks which meters are on a pole, or wants to check status of meters at a particular pole location. Provide the pole reference number (printed on the pole label).

grid_name.description: Grid name to narrow search (optional, helps if pole reference is ambiguous)

customer.name: customer_get_grid_status
customer.description: [READ-ONLY] Get comprehensive status for a single grid including power status (HPS/FS), capacity (kWp/kWh), DCU connectivity, and current weather. You MUST provide the grid_name parameter.

customer.name: customer_get_all_grids_status
customer.description: [READ-ONLY] Status dashboard for all accessible grids, including service status icons and weather.

customer.name: customer_get_last_gtr_summary
customer.description: [READ-ONLY] Get the last Grid Technical Report (GTR) summary for a specific grid. Returns KPI values, commentary, and pending issues from the most recent monthly review.

grid_name.description: Grid name to look up GTR for

customer.name: customer_get_fs_daily_summary
customer.description: [READ-ONLY] Get daily FS (Full Service) summary for a grid showing scheduled command executions vs actual state transitions. Includes daily FS ON hours, command delivery rates, and discrepancy detection. Supports arbitrary date ranges (max 30 days, defaults to yesterday + today).

start_date.description: Start date YYYY-MM-DD (inclusive, defaults to yesterday)

end_date.description: End date YYYY-MM-DD (inclusive, defaults to today)

customer.name: check_payment_completion
customer.description: [READ-ONLY] Check the completion status of a payment transaction. Provide the transaction reference exactly as given by the customer from their receipt or records to see if the payment was successful on the payment processor, whether the order is marked as completed, and the status of any associated directive (meter token). Do NOT construct or guess transaction references — they must come from the customer.

transaction_reference.description: Transaction reference exactly as provided by the customer from their receipt or records. Do NOT construct or guess this value.

customer.name: retry_commissioning
customer.description: [ACTION - STARTS METER COMMISSIONING] Retry a failed or stuck meter commissioning. Looks up the last commissioning attempt and re-triggers it via the platform. Only works if the previous attempt was not successful and no retry is already in progress. Only call when the user has explicitly requested this action.

meter_number.description: The meter number (external reference) to retry commissioning for

user_email.description: Email of the requesting user (injected by orchestrator)

customer.name: unassign_meter
customer.description: [ACTION - UNASSIGNS METER] Unassign a meter from its current connection. This removes the meter from the customer it is assigned to. Typically the first step in a reassignment flow — after unassigning, the meter must be manually reconnected to a new customer. Use when a meter is assigned to the wrong customer or needs to be decommissioned. Only call when the user has explicitly requested this action.

meter_number.description: The meter number (external reference) to unassign

user_email.description: Email of the requesting user (injected by orchestrator)

customer.name: set_meter_power_limit
customer.description: [ACTION - SETS HPS POWER LIMIT] Set the maximum power limit (HPS threshold) for a meter. Sends a SET_POWER_LIMIT interaction to the meter via the platform. Only the values listed in the enum are supported: 200W = standard HPS customer, 600W = high power tariff upgrade. Change takes effect on the next meter communication cycle. Only call when the user has explicitly requested this action.

power_limit_watts.description: Power limit in watts. 200 = standard HPS, 600 = high power. NOTE: if CUSTOMER_METER_POWER_LIMIT_OPTIONS env var is changed, update this enum to match.

user_email.description: Email of the requesting user (injected by orchestrator)

customer.name: resend_meter_token
customer.description: [ACTION - RESENDS METER TOKEN] Resend the last TOP_UP prepayment token to a meter. Looks up the most recent TOP_UP directive token and delivers it again. Only resends top-up (credit) tokens — not power limit or other control tokens. Use when a customer did not receive or lost their top-up token. Only call when the user has explicitly requested this action.

user_email.description: Email of the requesting user (injected by orchestrator)

customer.name: find_payment
customer.description: [READ-ONLY] Search for a payment order by customer name and approximate details (amount, date, organization). Use when the exact transaction reference is not available — e.g., working from a payment app screenshot. Returns matching orders; if exactly one match is found, automatically verifies it with the payment processor.

customer_name.description: Customer name from receipt or screenshot

date.description: Date or datetime from receipt in ISO format, e.g. 2026-01-30 (optional)

organization_name.description: Organization name if visible on receipt (optional)

schedule.name: schedule_user_command
schedule.description: Schedule a message (slash command or plain text) to run later or on a recurring basis (e.g., 'daily at 9am', 'every other monday at 9am', 'monthly on the 1st at 9am').

message.description: The message to schedule. Can be a slash command like '/tickets' or any regular text like 'show me the tickets assigned to anyone'

schedule.name: list_user_schedules
schedule.description: List all active scheduled commands for the current chat. Shows schedule ID, command, timing, and next run time.

schedule.name: cancel_user_schedule
schedule.description: Cancel a scheduled command by its ID. The ID can be found using list_user_schedules.

schedule_id.description: The schedule ID to cancel (UUID format or first 8 characters)

schedule.name: pause_user_schedule
schedule.description: Pause a recurring schedule. Can be resumed later.

schedule.name: create_user_agent
schedule.description: [ACTION] Create a persistent monitoring agent that checks a condition on a schedule and notifies the user when it's met. Use when the user says things like 'alert me when X', 'let me know if Y', 'watch Z for me', 'track when X happens'. Do NOT use for simple recurring commands (use schedule_schedule_user_command instead). BEFORE calling this tool, verify FEASIBILITY: (1) Can the check_prompt be answered using the tools currently available to you? If not, tell the user what's missing instead of creating an agent. (2) Does the entity exist? (e.g., if they mention a grid name, verify it's a real grid). (3) Is the data source accessible? (e.g., battery SOC requires VRM tools). If any check fails, explain why the agent can't be created and suggest alternatives. You MUST provide TWO prompts: (1) check_prompt — a yes/no gate question (e.g., 'Have any customers in ExampleGrid crossed 200W?'), and (2) response_prompt — the full detail query to run ONLY when the check triggers (e.g., 'List all customers in ExampleGrid above 200W with their meter ID and current power.'). Strip 'alert me', 'let me know', 'watch for' etc. from BOTH prompts. NEVER include 'create', 'agent', 'monitor' in either prompt.

agent_type.description: Agent type. Use 'condition_monitor' (default) for threshold alerts and watch-for-X requests. For other agent types defined in the expert instructions (e.g., site_visit_tracker, project_manager), use the expert_id from the doc. If unsure, use 'condition_monitor'.

check_prompt.description: For condition_monitor agents: YES/NO gate question evaluated every wake. Must end in '?'. For other agent types: the main task description or goal.

response_prompt.description: For condition_monitor: full detail query when check triggers. For other agent types: additional context or desired output format. Can be empty for expert-defined agents.

anchor_entity.description: Optional: name of the grid, organization, or other entity this agent is about. Used to enrich agent context with relevant chat history, status data, etc. Examples: 'ExampleGrid' (grid), 'ExampleOrg' (org). Supports fuzzy matching. Omit if the agent is not entity-specific.

wake_schedule.description: Cron expression for check frequency. Default: '0 8-18 * * 1-5' (hourly during work hours). Use '*/30 8-18 * * 1-5' for every 30 min, '0 9,13,17 * * 1-5' for 3x daily.

auto_complete.description: If true, agent auto-terminates after condition is met once. Default true for one-time alerts, false for ongoing tracking.

model_tier.description: Model tier: 'standard' for simple threshold checks, status monitoring, or factual lookups (e.g., 'alert me when SOC drops below 60%'). 'pro' for complex analysis, multi-step reasoning, regulatory interpretation, trend analysis, or tasks requiring deep domain understanding (e.g., 'track project development and coordinate site visits'). Default: 'standard'.

schedule.name: list_user_agents
schedule.description: [READ-ONLY] List all persistent monitoring agents created by the current user. Shows agent name, status, what it's checking, wake count, and last wake time.

include_terminated.description: Include cancelled agents in the list

schedule.name: cancel_user_agent
schedule.description: [ACTION] Cancel (terminate) a user-created monitoring agent. The agent stops checking and is marked as terminated. Use when user says 'cancel agent', 'stop monitoring', 'remove agent', or picks an agent to cancel from the list.

instance_id.description: UUID of the agent instance to cancel (from list_user_agents)

schedule.name: start_expert_workflow
schedule.description: [ACTION] Start an expert workflow in the background. Returns immediately with a packet_id. The agent will be woken when the workflow completes. Use check_workflow_result to read outputs. Available experts: lpp_expert (layout generation), gtr_expert (grid technical review). IMPORTANT: Provide ALL required inputs upfront — the workflow runs without user interaction.

inputs.description: JSON object with all required inputs for the workflow (e.g., '{"site_name": "ExampleGrid", "editable_total_buildings": 150}')

prefilled_inputs.description: Optional: JSON object with pre-filled responses for interactive steps. Format: '{"step_name": "response"}'

schedule.name: check_workflow_result
schedule.description: [READ-ONLY] Check the status and result of a previously started expert workflow. Returns packet status (pending/in_progress/completed/failed), outputs if completed, or error details if failed.

knowledge.name: summarize_knowledge
knowledge.description: Search the internal knowledge base for structured documentation on technical procedures.

knowledge.name: get_grid_review_history
knowledge.description: [READ-ONLY] Get monthly Grid Technical Review (GTR) history for a grid. Returns distilled monthly reviews with KPIs (CUF, losses, revenue collection, connections), commentary, actions taken, and pending issues. This is the monthly technical review summary — NOT live data. For live KPIs use Grafana tools, for recent O&M chat history use customer_get_grid_chat_chronology. Supports fuzzy grid name matching.

months_back.description: Number of months to look back (default 6, max 24)

knowledge.name: web_search
knowledge.description: [READ-ONLY] Search the web for current information not in the knowledge base. Use for: recent regulations or policy changes, current news, cultural/religious dates (Ramadan, Eid, holidays), market prices, or any question requiring up-to-date web information. Supports country targeting (default: Nigeria) and African energy domain prioritization.

query.description: Search query. Be specific — include country, year, and topic for best results.

country.description: Two-letter country code to bias results (e.g., 'ng' for Nigeria, 'cd' for DRC). Optional.

days_back.description: Limit results to the last N days (e.g., 7 for last week, 30 for last month). Optional.

include_domains.description: Domain filter preset: 'african_energy' (Nigerian/DRC news + regulatory), 'mini_grid' (mini-grid sector sources — AMDA, ESMAP, REA, IRENA, etc.), 'all' (both combined). Omit for general web.

knowledge.name: web_extract
knowledge.description: [READ-ONLY] Extract clean text content from a specific URL. Use when you have a URL (from search results or user-provided) and need to read the full page content. Returns cleaned text, not raw HTML.

url.description: Full URL to extract content from

knowledge.name: find_document
knowledge.description: Search Google Drive for a document by name fragment or document code (e.g., 'DOC-1234', 'ExampleSite Technical Review'). Returns the document name, URL, and Google Doc ID if exactly one match is found. If multiple matches are found, returns an error listing them so the user can provide a specific link. Use this when a user references a document by name or code and you need its ID for editing or reading.

query.description: Document name fragment or code to search for (e.g., 'DOC-1234', 'ExampleSite Grids Technical Review')

knowledge.name: read_document
knowledge.description: Read the full content of a Google Doc as markdown. Use this to understand the document's structure, style, and existing content before editing. Requires a document ID — use find_document first if you only have a name.

document_id.description: Google Doc file ID (from find_document or a Google Docs URL)

knowledge.name: scan_doc_comments
knowledge.description: Scan a Google Doc for pending @anansibot comments. Returns a list of comments with their highlighted text, instruction, and comment ID. Use this before edit_doc_section to see what edits are requested in a document.

document_id.description: Google Doc file ID (required — not a document name). If the user gives a name, use find_document first to resolve the ID.

knowledge.name: edit_doc_section
knowledge.description: Edit a section of a Google Doc with formatted markdown. SAFETY: This is a destructive write operation. Before calling: (1) confirm with the user which document and section will be edited, (2) never assume a document ID from context — require an explicit file ID. If the user provides a name, use find_document first. If find_document returns 2+ results, ask the user which one. Supports: **bold**, *italic*, ## headings, - bullets, 1. numbered lists, | tables |, [links](url).

document_id.description: Google Doc file ID (required — not a document name)

comment_id.description: Comment ID from scan_doc_comments (for comment-driven editing)

instruction.description: Edit instruction (for instruction-driven editing, or to override the comment text)

section_text.description: Exact text of the section to edit (for instruction-driven mode without comment_id)

replacement_markdown.description: Markdown-formatted replacement content. If not provided, the bot will generate it from the instruction.

messaging.name: messaging_send_to_group
messaging.description: Send a message to a registered staff Telegram group. Only groups defined in the Staff Groups configuration are allowed. Use this for escalations, cross-grid coordination, or posting summaries to specialized groups.

chat_id.description: Telegram chat ID of the target group (must be a registered staff group)

topic_id.description: Optional topic/thread ID for forum groups

reference.name: lookup_tariff
reference.description: [READ-ONLY] Look up Nigeria import tariff by item description or CET code. Accepts fuzzy item descriptions (e.g. 'breeding horses') or CET codes with or without dots (e.g. '0101.21.00.00' or '0101210000'). Returns VAT, levy (LVY), excise (EXC), and date of validity (DOV) from the Nigeria Customs import tariff schedule. NOTE: Rates are based on the 2022 Nigeria Customs tariff schedule and may not reflect subsequent policy changes or exemptions.

query.description: Item description or CET code (dots optional)

reference.name: get_import_prohibition_list
reference.description: [READ-ONLY] Fetch the current Nigeria import prohibition list from the Nigeria Customs Service website. Returns all categories of absolutely prohibited imports.

reference.name: lookup_import_standard
reference.description: [READ-ONLY] Look up Nigeria import standards by item name. Fuzzy-matches against the Nigeria Import Standards document and returns the applicable HS codes and remarks (standard reference, e.g. NIS 54:2017).
